heading: Local development districts and organizations
label: 15505 Local development districts and organizations
notes: Codificationand enacted identical sections. was repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246
Effective DateSection effective on the first day of the first fiscal year beginning after , see , set out as a note under .June 18, 2008section 14217(d) of Pub. L. 110–246section 15101 of this title

children.heading: .—Grants to Local Development Districts
children.label: a .—Grants to Local Development Districts
children.text: Subject to the requirements of this section, a Commission may make grants to a local development district to assist in the payment of development planning and administrative expenses.

children.heading: .—Maximum amount
children.label: 1 .—Maximum amount
children.text: The amount of a grant awarded under this section may not exceed 80 percent of the administrative and planning expenses of the local development district receiving the grant.

children.heading: .—Maximum period for state agencies
children.label: 2 .—Maximum period for state agencies
children.text: In the case of a State agency certified as a local development district, a grant may not be awarded to the agency under this section for more than 3 fiscal years.

children.heading: .—Local share
children.label: 3 .—Local share
children.text: The contributions of a local development district for administrative expenses may be in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated, including space, equipment, and services.
operate as a lead organization serving multicounty areas in the region at the local level;
assist the Commission in carrying out outreach activities for local governments, community development groups, the business community, and the public;
serve as a liaison between State and local governments, nonprofit organizations (including community-based groups and educational institutions), the business community, and citizens; and
assist the individuals and entities described in paragraph (3) in identifying, assessing, and facilitating projects and programs to promote the economic development of the region.
